title: pilosella macrostolona subsp. cernuiformis
intro: Pilosella macrostolona subsp. cernuiformis, also known as a rare subspecies of the Pilosella genus, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its unique, cernuiform (pivoting or bent) leaves and yellow flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and inflammation, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Pilosella macrostolona subsp. cernuiformis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Pilosella macrostolona subsp. cernuiformis often involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to leverage its therapeutic properties.
